title: No Game No Life (Volumes 1, 2, 9)
description: No Game No Life (Volumes 1, 2, 9) is a novel by Yuu Kamiya. Light novel volumes banned in Australia due to their depiction, "in a way that is likely to cause offence to a reasonable adult, a person who is, or appears to be, a child under 18".
descriptionParagraphs: No Game No Life (Volumes 1, 2, 9) is a novel by Yuu Kamiya. Light novel volumes banned in Australia due to their depiction, "in a way that is likely to cause offence to a reasonable adult, a person who is, or appears to be, a child under 18".

The surviving record is interesting because it shows how even ordinary-looking books can acquire a charged political afterlife. As a novel, it can be read not only for subject matter but for the way form, tone, and circulation make a text feel dangerous, intimate, or politically usable to anxious officials.

It also matters as part of a wider censorship history in Australia. The present page is a dossier starter built from source-tracked ban records; the surviving note currently says: Light novel volumes banned in Australia due to their depiction, "in a way that is likely to cause offence to a reasonable adult, a person who is, or appears to be, a child under 18". More publication history, translations, and close reading can be added later.

banningParagraphs: No Game No Life (Volumes 1, 2, 9) entered censorship debates as a novel associated with controversy, publication history, and state scrutiny. In the current dossier, the main state objections cluster around political sensitivity.

The earliest event currently captured here is 2020 in Australia, where Australia authorities banned publication or circulation. Light novel volumes banned in Australia due to their depiction, "in a way that is likely to cause offence to a reasonable adult, a person who is, or appears to be, a child under 18". counterReadings.title: 100 Banned Books: Censorship Histories of World Literature
counterReadings.author: Nicholas J. Karolides, Margaret Bald, and Dawn B. Sova
counterReadings.note: A compact reference on how censorship systems moved across states, churches, and courts.

counterReadings.title: Banned Books: 387 B.C. to 1978 A.D.
counterReadings.author: Anne Lyon Haight
counterReadings.note: Useful for comparing older obscenity, heresy, and political bans with modern free-speech disputes.
